verdict: DEPTH IS REAL IN BEHAVIOR, INVISIBLE IN THE BRAIN. Deep LLMs DO capture human conceptual structure beyond shallow co-occurrence -- on clean behavioral data they add 12.8% unique over GloVe (LLM 0.613 > GloVe 0.545). BUT that depth does NOT reach the brain: GloVe predicts the brain as well as the deep LLM (0.18 vs 0.182), deep-unique ~0.4%. So 'deep LLMs UNIQUELY predict the brain' is NOT supported at fMRI resolution -- a 2014 GloVe matches it equally. The deep advantage is real in concepts, absent (or unresolvable) in the neural signal.
honest_limit: All sources are human-derived; this separates shallow-language from deeper-conceptual, but cannot test substrate-independence.
